architecture_fit.note: Single QK head, score=qᵀMk, no MLP/value/output — a minimal delta from base_model.py that directly produces a sharp sign threshold (α≈14, θ≈0).

baseline_comparison.note: Linear max(0,cos) baseline gives lift 2.68, and two causal ablations (M→0, off-diagonal-only) are plotted as failing strawmen against the trained curve.

faithfulness.note: Zeroing M collapses the sweep to 0 and off-diagonal-only M flattens to ~0.5, while M≈s·I (cos(vec(M),vec(I))=0.992) — genuine causal evidence the diagonal circuit drives the flip.

operating_range.note: Full cosine axis (−1..1, 21 pts + per-slice slopes) is covered, but inputs stay unit-norm and no input-scale/noise robustness sweep is shown (the scale_robustness test from earlier runs was dropped).

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Attempt is trained, not hand-set, but it strongly demonstrates the learned M reduces to a hand-writable scaled identity (s·I); the hand-built sibling lives in pass_3.

visual_judgement.note: Demo leads with trained-vs-ablations sweep on the benchmark's own axes, plus a clean diagonal M heatmap and a live GPU histogram explorer — the claim is legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties y=mean attention (what the benchmark scores) and x=cosine to the claim, with ablations as the faithfulness evidence and the heatmap verifying M≈s·I.

notes: A clean, well-scoped trained-circuit attempt: a single learned bilinear QK head (no MLP) rediscovers the dot product (M≈s·I), yields a sharp threshold at cos=0 (α≈14, θ≈0), and beats the linear baseline 2.68×. Faithfulness is strong via M→0 and off-diagonal ablations through the same evaluator, and the visualisation makes all of this legible. Main gap is operating range — only the canonical unit-norm cosine sweep is exercised, with no input-scale or noise robustness test.
